Claude Code Routines: How to Set Up /schedule (and Why It Pushes to claude/ Instead of main)

I scheduled a task with Claude Code's /schedule, came back to the repo next day, and asked myself: I told it to push to main, why are there a bunch of claude/xxx branches instead? This post walks through Claude Code's three scheduling options (Routines, Desktop scheduled tasks, /loop), how /schedule works on the CLI, Routines' three trigger types (Schedule, API, GitHub event), custom cron rules, the 1-hour minimum interval — and most importantly, why pushes land on claude/-prefixed branches and how to flip "Allow unrestricted branch pushes" to fix that confusing behavior.
Why CSS overflow: scroll Shows No Scrollbar on macOS Chrome (vs. Windows)

You set overflow: scroll, then realize Mac Chrome shows no scrollbar — but your coworker on Windows sees one fine. This post starts with the four overflow values, then explains macOS's Overlay Scrollbar mechanism — why Chrome on macOS doesn't always show a scrollbar even with overflow: scroll. Three fixes: ::-webkit-scrollbar to force it, the newer standard scrollbar-width and scrollbar-color, and flipping macOS's "Show scroll bars" system setting to "Always".
VSCode Opens Every Link in the Integrated Browser? How to Disable Auto Takeover

VSCode 1.109 shipped a new Integrated Browser, and once workbench.browser.openLocalhostLinks is on, every localhost link in the terminal or chat view gets pulled into the editor — tiny pane, painful to use. This post covers how to disable the two key settings (workbench.browser.openLocalhostLinks and simpleBrowser.useIntegratedBrowser), and how to still call the integrated browser manually from the command palette when you actually want it.
Vibe Coding's 2026 Upgrade: A Six-Stage Agentic Engineering SOP with Templates and a Real Case

I've written about what Vibe Coding is and the basics newcomers should pick up. This post is the 2026 upgrade — the full playbook. In April 2026 Karpathy himself, at the Sequoia Ascent talk, upgraded vibe coding into agentic engineering. So this post takes the agentic engineering discipline and rebuilds the AI development workflow from scratch — three-layer documentation (Spec / Plan / Tasks via CLAUDE.md + AGENTS.md, ROADMAP, tasks.md templates), AI employee division of labor (Skill vs Sub-agent vs Agent Teams), development discipline (tests in the same turn, UI delegation, Conventional Commits), two-layer wrap-up validation (typecheck + test + build, plus Writer/Reviewer mode and AI citation cross-checks), a rollback decision table, and a two-tier Memory + CLAUDE.md architecture. The whole thing runs end-to-end against a personal e-commerce MVP (Nuxt 3 + Supabase + Stripe), including how to defuse the classic Stripe-checkout client-price-tampering security trap.
Fixing 'Blocked request' When Exposing a Nuxt 3 Dev Server Through ngrok

A quick note on fixing "Blocked request. This host is not allowed" when exposing a Nuxt 3 dev server through ngrok — including why Nuxt points you at a `vite.config.js` that doesn't exist, where to actually put `server.allowedHosts` in `nuxt.config.ts`, how to use the leading-dot wildcard to cover all ngrok subdomains, and why setting `allowedHosts: true` is a bad idea.
No Preview Image When You Share to LINE or Discord? Force-Refresh Social OG Cache with ?v=1

This post starts with the classic problem of sharing your blog post to a social platform and getting a stale preview image, then walks through Open Graph tags, how platforms cache og:image / og:title / og:description, why Facebook / X / LINE / Discord still show old content after you update your OG tags, and how to force a refresh by appending a cache-busting query string like `?v=1` `?v=2`. Also covers how to use the Facebook Sharing Debugger, rough cache lifetimes by platform, what to do about Twitter / X's Card Validator being retired in 2022, and whether `?v=1` impacts SEO or canonical URLs.
Cloudflare Pages Preview URLs Stay Live Forever? Lock Them Down with Cloudflare Access

Every Cloudflare Pages deployment gets a hash-based preview URL like `d682e28c.your-project.pages.dev`. This post starts with the security problem of those URLs persisting forever, then walks through how to use Cloudflare Access (free for up to 50 users) to put a Self-hosted application policy in front of them — locking the previews behind an "Emails ending in" rule. Covers the step-by-step setup, how to write OR rules in Access policies, and three things to check when the One-time PIN email doesn't arrive.
What's RSS? Why Still Use It in 2026? Plus the Chrome Extension I Wrote — Raydar

This post walks through what RSS actually is, how it differs from Newsletter subscriptions, why it's still worth using in 2026, how it compares to algorithm-driven feeds, what Atom is — and then I talk about Raydar, a Chrome RSS reader extension I wrote. It covers RSS auto-detection, one-click subscribe, three reading modes, local-first storage, no account required, OPML import/export, JSON backup, keyboard shortcuts — plus how to install it and why I bothered writing my own.
localhost vs 127.0.0.1: Engineer Lore, RFC History, and 'Connection Refused' Explained

This post covers localhost and 127.0.0.1 end to end — hostname resolution, what loopback is, the historical bits about 127.0.0.0/8 from RFC 790 to RFC 1122, and the 2005 IRC story Slashdot called "The Planet's Most Moronic Hacker" — the German bitchchecker incident where attacking 127.0.0.1 took down his own machine. The second half is a checklist for the most common reasons you get "localhost refused to connect" — dev server not running, port already taken, HSTS forcing HTTPS, hosts file weirdness, IPv6 ::1 resolution conflicts, and firewall stuff.
How to Use GPT-5.5 and ChatGPT Images 2.0: 30 Prompt Templates for IG Cards, Knowledge Decks, and Product Mockups

OpenAI shipped GPT-5.5 and ChatGPT Images 2.0 in the same week of late April 2026. GPT-5.5 is the new language model that handles understanding and reasoning; ChatGPT Images 2.0 (codename gpt-image-2) is the dedicated image generation model. The leap in image generation this round comes from those two working together — text rendering finally doesn't blur out, and Chinese, English, and Japanese all come out correct. This post clarifies how the two models split the work, then walks through what's new in GPT-5.5, what makes ChatGPT Images 2.0 strong, and ends with 30 copy-paste prompt templates across IG cards, knowledge decks, carousel covers, blog headers, infographics, product mockups, quote cards, and itineraries.
